sense.definition: the sensory experience of a pleasing or sugary flavor

examples.x: The sweetness of the ripe strawberries made them a delightful snack.
In baking, a touch of sweetness from honey enhances the flavor of the dessert.
The sweetness of the tropical fruit punch made it a favorite among the party guests.
Grandma's cookies were cherished for their perfect balance of sweetness and warmth.
The natural sweetness of the freshly squeezed orange juice added a refreshing twist to breakfast.

sense.definition: the sharp or tangy flavor often associated with acidic or tart substances

examples.x: The sourness of the lemonade made it a refreshing summer drink.
The natural sourness of the green apple added a crisp and tangy element to the salad.
The yogurt had a pleasant sourness, creating a delicious contrast in the dessert.
The vinaigrette dressing had a perfect balance of olive oil and vinegar, providing a delightful sourness to the salad.
She puckered her lips at the unexpected sourness of the unripe berry.

sense.definition: the characteristic taste sensation associated with the presence of salt in food

examples.x: The saltiness of the sea air added to the experience of walking along the beach.
The olives provided a burst of saltiness in the Mediterranean salad.
The chef adjusted the recipe to achieve the right level of saltiness in the soup.
The saltiness of the pretzels complemented the sweetness of the chocolate.
She enjoyed the saltiness of the roasted nuts as a savory snack.

sense.definition: the sharp and unpleasant taste often found in coffee, dark chocolate, or certain vegetables

examples.x: The bitterness of the black coffee lingered on her palate.
The baker balanced the sweetness of the cake with a hint of bitterness from cocoa.
The bitterness of the medicine made it challenging to swallow.
The herbal tea had a soothing warmth with a subtle bitterness.
The bitterness of the tonic water surprised her as she took a sip.

sense.definition: ‌a taste that is not sour, bitter, salty, or sweet, found in some foods such as meat, etc.

examples.x: The soy sauce added a burst of umami to the stir-fry, enhancing its overall taste.

sense.definition: the strong, sharp taste or sensation often associated with spicy or intensely flavored foods

examples.x: The pungency of the onions brought a bold flavor to the curry.
A small amount of wasabi added just the right level of pungency to the sushi.
The aged cheese was appreciated for its strong aroma and pungency.
The mustard's pungency heightened the overall taste of the sandwich.
The hot peppers contributed to the pungency of the salsa, creating a spicy kick.

sense.definition: the quality of having a strong or fiery taste due to the presence of spices or chili peppers

examples.x: The spiciness of the curry added a delightful kick to the dish.
Some people enjoy the spiciness of hot salsa with their chips.
The spiciness of the ginger in the stir-fry brought depth and warmth to the flavors.
The chef carefully balanced the spiciness in the chili to suit different taste preferences.
The spiciness of the Thai soup left a lingering warmth on the palate.

sense.definition: the level of spiciness or heat in food

examples.x: The hotness of the salsa took everyone by surprise at the party.
Adjusting the amount of chili powder in the curry controlled the level of hotness.
The chef's specialty dish was known for its perfect balance of hotness and savory flavors.
Some people prefer a milder hotness in their dishes, while others enjoy intense spiciness.
The hotness of the buffalo wings challenged even the most adventurous eaters.

sense.definition: a refreshing, mild, or soothing sensation in the mouth, often associated with mint, menthol, or cucumber
the dry, puckering, or rough sensation experienced in the mouth, often caused by substances like tannins in tea or unripe fruits

examples.x: The astringency of the red wine left a dry and puckering sensation on the palate.
Green tea is known for its astringency, providing a crisp and refreshing taste.
The unripe persimmon had a noticeable astringency that softened as it ripened.
The astringency of the unfiltered cider contributed to its distinctive and strong flavor.
The astringency of the blackberries added complexity to the fruity jam.

sense.definition: a taste, aroma, or sensation resembling that of metal such as certain medicines or amalgam dental fillings
(of flavour) tangy and sour, often due to the presence of acid

examples.x: The lemon juice gave the dish an acidic kick that brightened the flavors.
Her face puckered from the acidic taste of the green apple.
The wine was too acidic for my taste, leaving a sharp aftertaste.
The salad dressing had an acidic flavor that complemented the fresh vegetables.
He added a splash of vinegar to the sauce, making it more acidic and zesty.

sense.definition: having a sharp, bitter, or sour taste

examples.x: The astringent flavor of green tea leaves a refreshing and slightly bitter taste on the palate.
Some unripe fruits, such as persimmons, have a notably astringent taste that fades as they ripen.
A well-brewed black coffee can have an astringent edge, especially if it is made with dark roast beans.
Certain types of red apples have a mildly astringent flavor that contrasts with the sweetness of the fruit.
Astringent notes in dark chocolate can contribute to its complexity, adding a bitter and drying sensation.

sense.definition: having a strong taste that is unpleasant and not sweet

examples.x: The bitter taste of black coffee lingered on her tongue after she took a sip.
He made a face at the bitter medicine, finding it difficult to swallow.
He winced at the bitter aftertaste of the dark chocolate, preferring sweeter varieties.
The bitter flavor of the arugula salad was balanced by the sweetness of the balsamic dressing.
Despite its bitter taste, he appreciated the health benefits of eating kale in his salad.

sense.definition: having a taste that is a blend of both bitter and sweet flavors

examples.x: The bittersweet aftertaste of the almond biscotti complemented the coffee perfectly.
The dark caramel sauce had a truly bittersweet flavor, with intense bitterness and a luscious sweetness.
The dark roast coffee beans produced a bittersweet brew, blending a robust bitter kick with a nuanced caramel sweetness.

sense.definition: describing water that is slightly salty, typically where freshwater mixes with seawater

examples.x: The estuary is known for its brackish waters, which support a unique variety of wildlife.
The fisherman explained that certain fish thrive in brackish environments.
The scientists studied the effects of brackish water on local plant species.

sense.definition: (of food) having a spicy or peppery flavor that causes a burning sensation in the mouth

examples.x: The curry was so hot that I had to drink several glasses of water to cool down.
She enjoys hot salsa with her tacos for an extra kick.
The restaurant is known for its hot wings that challenge even the bravest eaters.
He accidentally added too much chili powder, making the stew unbearably hot.

sense.definition: having a flavor that is not sharp or overpowering

examples.x: The cheese had a mild flavor that everyone enjoyed.
He preferred mild sauces to avoid overwhelming the dish.
The tea was mild, with a subtle, soothing taste.
The mild taste of the bread complemented the rich soup perfectly.
She preferred a mild flavor, avoiding anything too spicy.

sense.definition: featuring a rich taste

examples.x: The robust coffee had a bold and rich flavor, leaving a lasting impression on the palate.
The hearty stew featured a robust blend of spices, creating a savory and satisfying taste.
The red wine boasted a robust profile, with a full-bodied and complex flavor.
The dark chocolate had a robust taste, combining intense bitterness with a deep, velvety sweetness.
The chef crafted a robust sauce, infusing the dish with a powerful and well-rounded flavor.

sense.definition: (of food) salty or spicy rather than sweet

examples.x: The savory sauce added depth of flavor to the pasta dish.
She cooked up a savory stir-fry with a variety of vegetables and tofu.
A bowl of savory miso soup warmed her up on the chilly evening.

sense.definition: (of food) flavored with spices, herbs, or other ingredients to improve its taste and smell

examples.x: The seasoned chicken was marinated in a blend of herbs and spices before grilling.
She prepared a seasoned pasta sauce with garlic, basil, and oregano.
The seasoned potatoes were roasted with rosemary and garlic until golden brown.
He enjoyed a seasoned steak, grilled to perfection with a savory rub.
They snacked on seasoned popcorn, sprinkled with chili powder and nutritional yeast.

sense.definition: describing a flavor that is intense and tangy, often with a biting or pungent quality

examples.x: The sharp flavor of the aged vinegar made the salad dressing stand out.
The sharp zest of the citrus fruit brightened up the otherwise bland dish.
The sharp tang of the sourdough starter gave the bread its distinctive taste.
The sharp tang of the pickled onions contrasted nicely with the sweetness of the carrots.

sense.definition: having a sharp acidic taste like lemon

examples.x: The green apple had a delightfully sour and crisp flavor.
He was surprised by the sour taste of the fruit.
She loves the sour taste of grapefruit in the morning.
The lemon juice was too sour for her to drink straight.

sense.definition: having a strong taste that gives your mouth a pleasant burning feeling

examples.x: She enjoyed the spicy curry with its blend of aromatic spices that lingered on her palate.
He couldn't handle the spicy chili sauce and reached for a glass of water to cool his burning mouth.
They ordered the spicy Thai noodles, craving the intense heat and bold flavors.

sense.definition: (of food) cooked in a way that tastes both sweet and sour
characterized by a taste that is somewhat sweet but not overwhelmingly so

examples.x: The sauce had a sweetish undertone, balancing the savory flavors of the dish.
The sweetish hint in the tea made it a gentle and soothing beverage.
The fruit salad was refreshing with its sweetish combination of various ripe fruits.
The barbecue sauce had a sweetish tang, adding a mild sweetness to the grilled meat.

sense.definition: having an overly sweet flavor
having a sharp taste that stings the tongue

examples.x: The lemon pie had a tart flavor that made my mouth pucker.
The yogurt was pleasantly tart and refreshing.
The cranberry sauce was a bit too tart for my taste.
A splash of lime juice added a tart kick to the margarita.

sense.definition: (of food)  not containing added salt

examples.x: The unsalted popcorn catered to those who preferred a plain and unseasoned snack.
The unsalted crackers provided a neutral base for pairing with various toppings.
The chef prepared unsalted butter cookies, allowing the sweetness to shine without added saltiness.
The soup was intentionally unsalted, allowing the individual flavors of the vegetables to come forward.
The unsalted nuts offered a simple and wholesome snack option.

sense.definition: having too much water and little taste

examples.x: The soup was disappointingly watery, lacking the rich flavor of homemade broth.
He complained that the pasta sauce was too watery, with not enough thickness and depth of flavor.
She added too much liquid to the batter, resulting in a watery consistency for the pancake mix.
The coffee tasted watery, as if it had been brewed with too much water and not enough coffee grounds.
The smoothie was watery and bland, lacking the creaminess and sweetness of properly blended fruit.

sense.definition: a sharp and distinctive taste, typically associated with acidity or a lively and refreshing quality

examples.x: The pineapple had a tropical tang, making it a juicy and flavorful fruit.
The buttermilk pancakes had a subtle tang that paired well with sweet maple syrup.
The fermented kimchi added a spicy tang to the Korean dish, enhancing its complexity.
The yogurt had a pleasant tang, balancing creaminess with a hint of acidity.
The pickles offered a crunchy texture along with a tang that complemented the sandwich.

sense.definition: the distinctive and enjoyable taste or aroma of food or drink

examples.x: The homemade soup had a rich and comforting savor that reminded me of childhood.
With each bite, the chocolate truffle released its decadent savor, delighting the taste buds.
The freshly baked bread filled the kitchen with a warm and inviting savor.
The herbal tea had a soothing savor, creating a calming experience with every sip.
The seafood risotto boasted a maritime savor, highlighting the freshness of the ocean ingredients.

sense.definition: the intense taste experience of food, particularly characterized by a sharp or strong flavor

examples.x: The chili had a fiery bite that lingered on the taste buds.
The aged cheese offered a complex bite, combining nuttiness with a sharp tang.
The hot sauce added a bold bite to the dish, leaving a spicy kick.
The black coffee had a robust bite, perfect for those who enjoy strong flavors.
The gingerbread had a sweet-spicy bite, making it a flavorful holiday treat.

sense.definition: the lingering flavor that remains in the mouth after eating or drinking something

examples.x: The espresso had a robust aftertaste, leaving a lingering hint of roasted coffee beans.
The garlic-infused dish had a savory aftertaste that subtly lingered on the tongue.
The rich dark chocolate had a bittersweet aftertaste that pleased the palate.
The minty freshness of the gum provided a cool and invigorating aftertaste.
The sweet and tangy fruit salad had a delightful aftertaste, making it a refreshing dessert option.

sense.definition: the state of having a delicious taste
a unique taste sensation that is attributed to the perception of fats or lipids in food
an individual who has a heightened sense of taste and is more sensitive to the flavors and sensations in food and beverages
something that one dislikes first but starts to like it with the passage of time
having the taste of salt

examples.x: The seafood stew had a rich and briny flavor, reminiscent of the maritime regions it originated from.
Wading in the shallows, the children felt the briny water on their skin as waves washed over the sand.
The briny taste of the oysters reminded diners of the ocean's influence on their flavor.
As the ship sailed through the briny waters, sailors could taste the salt on their lips.

sense.definition: having characteristics of soil or the earth, often associated with flavors such as mushrooms, root vegetables, or certain types of wine
having a sweet, fresh, or juicy taste or smell associated with various types of fruits
(of drinks) having a rich and intense flavor
having a taste or aroma reminiscent of nuts, often rich, earthy, and slightly sweet

examples.x: The granola bar had a nutty flavor, with a blend of almonds, oats, and honey.
The stir-fried vegetables had a nutty undertone from the addition of cashews.
The brown rice had a nutty aroma and taste, providing a wholesome side dish.
The pastry was filled with a nutty blend of pecans and caramel, creating a delicious dessert.
The pesto sauce had a nutty richness, combining basil, pine nuts, and Parmesan cheese.

sense.definition: containing a high amount of fat, sugar, or other indulgent ingredients

examples.x: The chocolate cake was incredibly rich, with layers of dense chocolate and a generous amount of frosting.
She enjoyed a rich Alfredo pasta, loaded with creamy sauce and grated cheese.
The dessert was rich and decadent, featuring a buttery crust and a filling of sweet caramel.
The dish was so rich that a small portion was enough to satisfy her, thanks to its creamy and flavorful ingredients.
He found the rich, buttery lobster bisque to be a delightful treat, full of deep, savory flavors.

sense.definition: having a taste like smoke

examples.x: The barbecue ribs had a deliciously smoky taste that lingered on the palate.
She added smoked paprika to the dish, giving it a subtle smoky flavor.
The smoky taste of the roasted vegetables added depth to the salad.
The cheese had a rich, smoky flavor from being aged in a wood-fired cellar.

sense.definition: having a sharp, refreshing taste with a slight sourness or acidity

examples.x: The tangy citrus flavor of the lemonade was refreshing on a hot day.
She enjoyed the tangy taste of the vinegar-based salad dressing on her greens.
The barbecue sauce was perfectly balanced, featuring a tangy blend of tomatoes, vinegar, and spices.
She savored the tangy taste of the pickles, relishing their crispness and vinegary punch.
The tangy yogurt sauce complemented the spicy kebabs perfectly.

sense.definition: having a taste or aroma resembling yeast, often found in bread, beer, or other fermented foods

examples.x: The homemade bread had a deliciously yeasty flavor, evoking warmth and freshness.
The pastry had a subtle yeasty undertone, contributing to its airy and light texture.
The sourdough pancakes had a tangy and yeasty flavor, offering a unique twist on a classic breakfast dish.
The croissant had a rich and yeasty flavor, making it a delightful breakfast treat.
The pizza dough had a distinctively yeasty taste, signaling its artisanal preparation.

sense.definition: (of food) having a sharp, strong, and refreshing taste

examples.x: She prepared a zesty marinade for the chicken, combining lemon juice, garlic, and herbs.
The zesty lemonade was refreshing on a hot summer day, with its tart and citrusy taste.
He enjoyed the zesty kick of the spicy chili sauce on his sandwich.
The zesty salad dressing, made with balsamic vinegar and Dijon mustard, brought the greens to life.

sense.definition: describing a flavor or aroma that is reminiscent of wood, often earthy, rich, and sometimes slightly sweet or spicy
